license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.4rvgv66
title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol 7: pBIOZOL-LiCl Method

steps_raw.instruction: Grind tissue to a powder in liquid nitrogen.
Add 1.3 mL of cold ( 4 °C ) pBIOZOL reagent for up to 100 mg gram of frozen, ground tissue.
Incubate the tube for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Lay the tube down horizontally to maximize surface area during RNA extraction.
Centrifuge at for 00:02:00 .
Transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5 ml RNase-free tube.
Add 50 µL of 2 Molarity (M) NaAc (pH 4.2) to the extract.
Centrifuge the mixture at 4 °C for 00:10:00 at to separate the phases.
Add to the aqueous phase 1/3 volume of 10 Molarity (M) LiCl.
Centrifuge the mixture at 4 °C for 00:20:00 at > .
Decant the supernatant, taking care not to lose the pellet.
Centrifuge at 4 °C for 00:03:00 at > .
Repeat the previous two steps.
Add 50 µL RNase-free water to dissolve the RNA pellet.
Add SSTE buffer to RNA to a total volume of 600 µL .
Vortex the tube until the phases mix and appears cloudy.
Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 in a microcentrifuge.
Transfer the top, aqueous phase to a new 1.5 ml RNase-free tube.
Vortex the tube until the phases mix and appear cloudy.
Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 .
Transfer the top aqueous phase to a new 1.5 ml RNase-free tube.
Centrifuge at 4 °C for 00:20:00 at > .
Add 1 mL of 75 % ethanol to the pellet.
Centrifuge at 4 °C for 00:05:00 at .
Repeat step 18 and 19.
Open cap and air-dry the pellet no more than 00:05:00 .
Add 30 µL RNase-free water to dissolve the pellet.
Before library construction, treat RNA with DNase I.
Mix by briefly vortexing or flicking the bottom of the tube until the sample is thoroughly suspended.
Tap tube to mix.
Then add 100 µL 5 Molarity (M) NaCl and 300 µL chloroform.
Vortex vigorously.
Transfer the top aqueous phase (about 400 µL – 500 µL ) to a new 1.5 ml RNase-free tube.
Mix and let stand at 4 °C overnight.
Add 1 mL of 75 % ethanol to the pellet.
Stand the tube at Room temperature for 00:03:00 . Pellet may be difficult to see.
Decant the liquid carefully, taking care not to lose the pellet.
Briefly centrifuge to collect the residual liquid and remove it with a pipette.
Pipette the water up and down over the pellet to dissolve the RNA. If you extract more than 100 mg plant tissues, combine different extractions to one tube. .
Then add equal volume of 25:24:1 acid phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol to the tube.
Then incubate at 20 °C for 00:05:00 .
Add equal volume of 24:1 chloroform:isoamyl alcohol to the tube.
Then incubate at 20 °C for 00:05:00 .
Add to the aqueous phase 2 volumes of 100 % ethanol, 1/10 volume of 3 Molarity (M) NaAc (pH 5.2) and 2 µL 5 mg/ml glycogen.
Invert tube to mix.
Store at -20 °C for 02:00:00 .
Decant the supernatant carefully to avoid losing the pellet.
Incubate at 20 °C for 00:03:00 .
Decant the liquid carefully, taking care not to lose the pellet.
Briefly centrifuge to collect the residual liquid and remove it with a pipette.
